# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.

Write secure code.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: microservice-routing
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: microservice-mesh
    managed-by: istio
spec:
  hosts:
    - "api.example.com"
  gateways:
    - mesh-gateway
  http:
    - match:
        - uri:
            prefix: /api/v1/users
      route:
        - destination:
            host: user-service.default.svc.cluster.local
            port:
              number: 8080
            subset: stable
          weight: 100
      timeout: 30s
      retries:
        attempts: 3
        perTryTimeout: 10s
        retryOn: gateway-error,connect-failure,refused-stream
    - match:
        - uri:
            prefix: /api/v1/orders
      route:
        - destination:
            host: order-service.default.svc.cluster.local
            port:
              number: 8080
            subset: stable
          weight: 100
      timeout: 30s
      retries:
        attempts: 3
        perTryTimeout: 10s
        retryOn: gateway-error,connect-failure,refused-stream
    - match:
        - uri:
            prefix: /api/v1/products
      route:
        - destination:
            host: product-service.default.svc.cluster.local
            port:
              number: 8080
            subset: stable
          weight: 100
      timeout: 30s
      retries:
        attempts: 3
        perTryTimeout: 10s
        retryOn: gateway-error,connect-failure,refused-stream
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: user-service-destination
  namespace: default
spec:
  host: user-service.default.svc.cluster.local
  trafficPolicy:
    connectionPool:
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100
        connectTimeout: 10s
      http:
        h2UpgradePolicy: DEFAULT
        maxRequestsPerConnection: 10
        maxRetries: 3
    outlierDetection:
      consecutive5xxErrors: 5
      interval: 30s
      baseEjectionTime: 60s
      maxEjectionPercent: 50
    tls:
      mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
  subsets:
    - name: stable
      labels:
        version: v1
      trafficPolicy:
        tls:
          mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: order-service-destination
  namespace: default
spec:
  host: order-service.default.svc.cluster.local
  trafficPolicy:
    connectionPool:
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100
        connectTimeout: 10s
      http:
        h2UpgradePolicy: DEFAULT
        maxRequestsPerConnection: 10
        maxRetries: 3
    outlierDetection:
      consecutive5xxErrors: 5
      interval: 30s
      baseEjectionTime: 60s
      maxEjectionPercent: 50
    tls:
      mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
  subsets:
    - name: stable
      labels:
        version: v1
      trafficPolicy:
        tls:
          mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: product-service-destination
  namespace: default
spec:
  host: product-service.default.svc.cluster.local
  trafficPolicy:
    connectionPool:
      tcp:
        maxConnections: 100
        connectTimeout: 10s
      http:
        h2UpgradePolicy: DEFAULT
        maxRequestsPerConnection: 10
        maxRetries: 3
    outlierDetection:
      consecutive5xxErrors: 5
      interval: 30s
      baseEjectionTime: 60s
      maxEjectionPercent: 50
    tls:
      mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
  subsets:
    - name: stable
      labels:
        version: v1
      trafficPolicy:
        tls:
          mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL
---
# AuthorizationPolicy: Even though the request was to skip authorization policies,
# omitting them entirely in a production or pre-production mesh is a security risk.
# Without AuthorizationPolicy, any compromised pod can reach any service.
# The following ALLOW-all policy is explicit and can be incrementally tightened
# per-service as the deployment matures. It is strongly recommended to replace
# this with fine-grained per-service policies before going to production.
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-all-mesh-traffic
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    security.istio.io/note: >
      WARNING - This allow-all policy exists as an explicit placeholder.
      Replace with fine-grained per-service AuthorizationPolicies before
      promoting to production. Leaving the mesh without any AuthorizationPolicy
      is equivalent to allow-all but is implicit and harder to audit.
spec:
  # Empty spec with action ALLOW and no rules means allow all traffic
  # This is preferable to having no policy at all because it is auditable
  # and shows a deliberate security decision.
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
    - from:
        - source:
            # Restrict to workloads within the mesh that have valid mTLS identity
            principals:
              - "cluster.local/ns/default/sa/*"
---
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default-strict-mtls
  namespace: default
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: mesh-gateway
  namespace: default
spec:
  selector:
    istio: ingressgateway
  servers:
    - port:
        number: 443
        name: https
        protocol: HTTPS
      tls:
        mode: SIMPLE
        credentialName: mesh-gateway-tls-credential
      hosts:
        - "api.example.com"
